actual_failure_evidence.fold0_launcher_tail: AssertionError in frozen exact calibration SHA256 equality assertion after both fold0 arm results existed

claim_boundary: Technical training-protocol failure only; no model-quality, OOF-performance, binding, Kd, experimental blocking, Docking Gold, Top7500, or promotion verdict.
monitoring_disclosure: Before root-cause identification, routine log monitoring displayed one C0 fold metric block. No aggregate OOF rows, held-out contact labels, open-development, sealed, quarantine, test32, or frozen prospective-test data were used. The V1.3 repair changes only artifact lifecycle and preserves every method and gate.
root_cause: C0 and C1 independently repeated the same floating GPU gradient calibration. All observed folds selected lambda=0.0025 with zero severe conflicts, but gradient norm/cosine decimals differed, producing different JSON bytes and SHA256. The frozen pair terminal requires exact calibration SHA equality.
v1_2_disposition: Frozen failed attempt. Never patch, resume, pool, select from, or reuse any V1.2 fold output.
v1_3_minimum_recovery: Materialize one shared content-addressed calibration artifact per fold before either arm optimizer; both fresh C0/C1 runs validate and replay the exact same bytes; rerun all ten arms from original seed43 initial state.
